# Team News: Team News: Mexico vs. Ecuador injury, suspension list, predicted XIs ahead of World Cup knockout clash

By Aishat Akanni| 28 Jun 2026 16:28

© Imago / ImagenShop

Mexico and Ecuador meet at the Mexico City Stadium on Wednesday morning in a Round of 32 showdown at this summer’s World Cup.

El Tri secured top spot in Group A after claiming a commanding 3-0 victory over Czech to cap off their group stage campaign, while Sebastin Beccacece's side progressed as third-place finishers following a dramatic 2-1 triumph over Germany in their final group match.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both nations ahead of Wednesday’s knockout clash.

---

## MEXICO vs. ECUADOR

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/mexico/world-cup-2026/preview/mexico-vs-ecuador-prediction-team-news-lineups_600278.html

## MEXICO

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI:

Rangel; Sanchez, Montes, Vasquez, Gallardo; Lira, Romo, Gutierrez; Alvarado, Jimenez, Quinones

## ECUADOR

Out:

news_items.title: Mexico vs Ecuador Preview, Lineups & Team News | RotoWire
news_items.text:
Mexico vs Ecuador Preview, Lineups & Team News | RotoWire

# Mexico vs Ecuador Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Round of 32

Mexico vs Ecuador clash in Mexico City for their 2026 World Cup Round of 32 knockout. Predicted lineups, team news, tactical breakdown and score prediction.

Updated on June 30, 2026 2:33AM EST

Share

By:

Pierre Courtin

Adam Zdroik

Updated on June 30, 2026 2:33AM EST

World Cup

From the latest news to creating the best starting lineup, we've got you covered - Sign-up today

# Mexico vs Ecuador Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Round of 32

Of all the Round of 32 ties, this is the one that should be tight and low-scoring. Mexico won Group A with three straight wins and three clean sheets, and they get to play this one at home in front of a packed Estadio Azteca. Ecuador limped through as one of the best third-placed teams, but they did it on the back of a genuinely excellent defense.

The story here is two strong back lines and two attacks that have flattered to deceive. Mexic

news_items.title: Who’ll be in Mexico’s starting lineup against Ecuador in the World Cup round of 32? - AS USA
news_items.text:
Who’ll be in Mexico’s starting lineup against Ecuador in the World Cup round of 32? - AS USA

Bryan Arellano Follow Bryan Arellano on X AS USA

Update: Jun 30th, 2026 02:34 EDT

0

Share

Go to facebook

Go to twitter

Go to whatsapp

Make us preferred on Google

Mexico wrapped up a flawless World Cup group stage, taking the full nine points on offer and, just as impressively, conceding zero goals - an unprecedented feat in El Tri’s record books. Now it’s time to turn the page and shift focus to what really matters: the knockout rounds.

The teams that advanced out of the group stage now know their path and what it will take to keep their FIFA tournament dreams alive. Mexico, led by a Javier Aguirre side that has seemingly gotten everything right so far, is preparing to welcome Ecuador to the iconic Estadio Azteca - a venue that has become a true fortress for the tournament co-hosts.

After Mexico rounded off Group A with a commanding 3-0 win over Czechia, Aguirre is now getting ready to make adjustments for the round of 32. It’s do-or-die from here on out: the winner moves on to the last 16, while the loser heads home.

Mexico vs Ecuador – Match preview and team news

Host nation Mexico is set to continue their FIFA World Cup journey with a Round of 32 Fixture against Ecuador on Wednesday at the Estadio Azteca.

## Mexico vs Ecuador – Match preview and team news

Date: Wednesday, 1st July.

Kick-off: 2am BST.

Venue: Mexico City Stadium, Ciudad de México.

## Mexico team news

Mexico enters this highly anticipated World Cup Round of 32 knockout match at the Estadio Azteca with a completely healthy roster.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Manager Javier Aguirre has no new injury concerns or suspensions, allowing him to select his absolute strongest starting eleven.

Striker Raúl Jiménez is highly anticipated to return to lead the frontline after being intentionally rested during the final group-stage victory over Czechia.

In goal, Raúl Rangel will resume his starting duties; iconic veteran Guillermo Ochoa returns to the bench after making a brief c

# Team News: Team News: Mexico vs. Ecuador injury, suspension list, predicted XIs ahead of World Cup knockout clash

By Aishat Akanni| 28 Jun 2026 16:28

© Imago / ImagenShop

Mexico and Ecuador meet at the Mexico City Stadium on Wednesday morning in a Round of 32 showdown at this summer’s World Cup.

El Tri secured top spot in Group A after claiming a commanding 3-0 victory over Czech to cap off their group stage campaign, while Sebastin Beccacece's side progressed as third-place finishers following a dramatic 2-1 triumph over Germany in their final group match.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both nations ahead of Wednesday’s knockout clash.

---

## MEXICO vs. ECUADOR

# Mexico vs Ecuador Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Round of 32

Of all the Round of 32 ties, this is the one that should be tight and low-scoring. Mexico won Group A with three straight wins and three clean sheets, and they get to play this one at home in front of a packed Estadio Azteca. Ecuador limped through as one of the best third-placed teams, but they did it on the back of a genuinely excellent defense.

The story here is two strong back lines and two attacks that have flattered to deceive. Mexic"}
{"title": "Who’ll be in Mexico’s starting lineup against Ecuador in the World Cup round of 32? - AS USA", "url": "https://en.as.com/soccer/world-cup/wholl-be-in-mexicos-starting-lineup-against-ecuador-in-the-world-cup-round-of-32-f202606-n/", "publishedDate": "2026-06-30T06:34:27.000Z", "author": null, "text": "Who’ll be in Mexico’s starting lineup against Ecuador in the World Cup round of 32? - AS USA

Bryan Arellano Follow Bryan Arellano on X AS USA

Update: Jun 30th, 2026 02:34 EDT

0

Mexico wrapped up a flawless World Cup group stage, taking the full nine points on offer and, just as impressively, conceding zero goals - an unprecedented feat in El Tri’s record books. Now it’s time to turn the page and shift focus to what really matters: the knockout rounds.

The teams that advanced out of the group stage now know their path and what it will take to keep their FIFA tournament dreams alive. Mexico, led by a Javier Aguirre side that has seemingly gotten everything right so far, is preparing to welcome Ecuador to the iconic Estadio Azteca - a venue that has become a true fortress for the tournament co-hosts.

After Mexico rounded off Group A with a commanding 3-0 win over Czechia, Aguirre is now getting ready to make adjustments for the round of 32. It’s do-or-die from here on out: the winner moves on to the last 16, while the loser heads home.

Mexico vs Ecuador – Match preview and team news

Host nation Mexico is set to continue their FIFA World Cup journey with a Round of 32 Fixture against Ecuador on Wednesday at the Estadio Azteca.

## Mexico vs Ecuador – Match preview and team news

Date: Wednesday, 1st July.

Kick-off: 2am BST.

Venue: Mexico City Stadium, Ciudad de México.

## Mexico team news

Mexico enters this highly anticipated World Cup Round of 32 knockout match at the Estadio Azteca with a completely healthy roster.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Manager Javier Aguirre has no new injury concerns or suspensions, allowing him to select his absolute strongest starting eleven.

Striker Raúl Jiménez is highly anticipated to return to lead the frontline after being intentionally rested during the final group-stage victory over Czechia.

In goal, Raúl Rangel will resume his starting duties; iconic veteran Guillermo Ochoa returns to the bench after making a brief c"}
